objective: Generate and identify equivalent fractions using multiplication and division patterns

learn.title: 곱하기 규칙 — 같은 수로 ×
learn.content: In L6 you used models to find equivalent fractions. Now let's find the PATTERN. Look: 1/2 = 2/4. What happened? Numerator: 1 × 2 = 2. Denominator: 2 × 2 = 4. Both were multiplied by 2! RULE: Multiply the numerator and denominator by the SAME number → you get an equivalent fraction. 예: Use the rule. 1/3 × 2/2 = ? → 2/6. (1 × 2 = 2 (numerator). 3 × 2 = 6 (denominator). So 1/3 = 2/6.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 나누기 규칙 — 같은 수로 ÷ (약분)
learn.content: You can also go the other direction. 4/8 → divide both by 4 → 1/2. RULE: Divide the numerator and denominator by the SAME number → equivalent fraction. This is called SIMPLIFYING. 4/8 simplifies to 1/2. 예: Simplify 6/8. Divide numerator and denominator by 2. → 3/4. (6 ÷ 2 = 3. 8 ÷ 2 = 4. So 6/8 = 3/4.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 동치 검증 — 비교 후 확인
learn.content: How to CHECK if two fractions are equivalent: Multiply or divide to see if one becomes the other. Example: Is 2/3 = 4/6? → 2 × 2 = 4 ✓, 3 × 2 = 6 ✓. Both multiplied by 2 → YES, equivalent. Example: Is 1/4 = 3/8? → 1 × 3 = 3 ✓, but 4 × 3 = 12 ≠ 8 ✗. NOT equivalent. 예: Is 2/6 equivalent to 1/3? Show why. → Yes. 2 ÷ 2 = 1, 6 ÷ 2 = 3. Both divided by 2 → 2/6 = 1/3.. (Divide numerator and denominator by the same number (2). You get 1/3. Equivalent!)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: n/n = 1 — 동치의 핵심
learn.content: 곱셈/나눗셈 규칙의 원리: 어떤 수에 1을 곱해도 변하지 않음.   2/2 = 1, 3/3 = 1, 4/4 = 1, …   분수에 n/n 곱 = 1 곱 = 그대로 (다른 표기). 예: 1/2 × 2/2 = 2/4 (값은 1/2 그대로). 🔍 핵심: 같은 양인데 더 잘게 표현.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 스케일 인자 찾기 — 분모 비율
learn.content: 1/3 = ?/9를 풀려면: 분모가 3에서 9로 → ×3. 분자도 같이 ×3 → 1×3 = 3. 답: 3/9.   단계: 분모 변화의 인자 찾기 → 분자에 같은 인자 곱하기. 예: 2/4 = ?/8 → 분모 ×2 → 분자도 ×2 → 4/8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 동치 만들기 3단계 루틴
learn.content: 주어진 분수의 동치 분수 만들기:   단계 1) 원하는 분모(또는 분자) 확인.   단계 2) 원본과 비교해 곱셈 인자 결정.   단계 3) 분자/분모에 같은 인자 곱하기. 예: 1/2 = ?/8 → 분모 ×4 → 1×4/2×4 = 4/8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 약분 3단계 루틴
learn.content: 분수를 더 간단히 (약분):   단계 1) 분자와 분모의 공약수 찾기.   단계 2) 공약수로 분자/분모 둘 다 나누기.   단계 3) 더 약분 안 되면 끝 (기약 분수). 예: 6/8 → ÷2 → 3/4. 더 약분 안 됨 → 3/4가 가장 간단.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 한쪽만 ×하면 다른 분수 — M03 함정
learn.content: 흔한 실수 (M03): 분자만 또는 분모만 곱/나눔.   잘못: 1/2 = 2/2 (분자만 ×2 → 다른 분수, 값 1)   잘못: 1/2 = 1/4 (분모만 ×2 → 더 작은 분수)   맞음: 1/2 = 2/4 (둘 다 ×2). 🔍 검산: 두 분수가 같은 모델/수직선 점에 매핑되는가?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Find an equivalent fraction: 1/4 = __/8
try.hint: Multiply numerator and denominator by the same number.
guided_steps: Step 1: Denominator went from 4 to 8. That's × 2.
Step 2: Multiply numerator by the same: 1 × 2 = 2.

try.question: Are 3/4 and 6/8 equivalent? How do you know?
try.hint: Check: does multiplying top and bottom by the same number work?
guided_steps: Step 3: Same multiplier (2) for both → equivalent.

practice_r2.question: Ava says 2/8 of a pizza is the same as 1/4 of the same pizza. Is she correct? Show why.
practice_r2.answer: Yes. 2/8 ÷ 2/2 = 1/4. Divide numerator and denominator by 2. 2/8 = 1/4.

practice_r3.question: Explain the rule for finding equivalent fractions in your own words. Give an example.
practice_r3.answer: Multiply (or divide) the numerator and denominator by the SAME number. This doesn't change the value of the fraction, only how it looks. Example: 1/2 → multiply both by 3 → 3/6. 1/2 = 3/6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Ben says 2/4 = 3/6 = 4/8. Is he correct? Prove it.
practice_r3.answer: Yes, all three equal 1/2. 2/4 ÷ 2/2 = 1/2. 3/6 ÷ 3/3 = 1/2. 4/8 ÷ 4/4 = 1/2. They all simplify to 1/2, so they are all equivalent.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.7 'Equivalent Fractions' pp.377-380
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G — Generating Equivalent Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Sara ate 4/6 of her granola bar. Tim ate 2/3 of his same-size granola bar. Sara says she ate more because 4 > 2. Is Sara correct? Explain.
practice_r3.answer: Sara is NOT correct. 4/6 and 2/3 are equivalent fractions. 4/6 ÷ 2/2 = 2/3. They ate the same amount. You can't just compare numerators when the denominators are different.
